observations.topic: REV0/REV1/REV2 defect-closure re-confirmed via independent oracle (task item 1)
observations.detail:
Built an oracle strictly from design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_normalization_finding's REV3 prose (frontmatter-strip -> python3 `<[^>]*>` -> ' ' tag-strip -> whitespace-collapse -> `re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+')` segmentation -> per-segment `vlib_no_stale_payload`, the REAL unmodified tools/verify_lib.sh primitive, invoked once per file/fixture). (1) rev0 co-located-sentence false-pass: reconstructed a stale-3-dir + unrelated-tools/ two-sentence paragraph (mirroring scope.files_create's stale_payload_cosentence.md description) -- oracle correctly splits into two segments and FAILs only on the stale one; the unrelated tools/ sentence never appears in the offending text. (2) rev1 false-negatives: reconstructed a 5-physical-line, no-interior-punctuation wrap (mirroring stale_payload_longwrap.md) and a `;`-broken 2-line enumeration (mirroring stale_payload_semicolon.md) -- both correctly FAIL, each reconstructed as one whole segment spanning all original physical lines, confirming the design never inspects line counts or treats `;`/`:` as terminators. (3) rev2 HTML `

` false-pass: reconstructed the described stale_payload_html.html content (a stale `

...only.

` immediately followed, same physical source line, no blank line, by an unrelated `

...tools/...

`) -- oracle correctly FAILs, isolating only the stale `

` sentence's text; tag-stripping-to-space exposes the `.`+whitespace boundary regardless of original line layout, exactly as claimed. All three prior rejection classes are genuinely closed by the redesign, not merely reasoned about.

observations.topic: Abbreviation-mid-enumeration segmentation risk (task item 2) -- probed, corpus-irrelevant, ruled non-blocking
observations.detail: Confirmed the theoretical risk is real in isolation: a contrived sentence such as 'The payload includes `standard/`, e.g. also `templates/`, and `schema/` only.' does fragment at the 'e.g.' + whitespace boundary and produces a false-PASS (oracle rc=0) because the split-off first fragment ('...includes `standard/`, e.g.') and second fragment ('also `templates/`, and `schema/` only.') each individually lack all three of standard/+templates/+schema/. However, an exhaustive grep of every real default-mode file (all 37 build_shipped_md_set() files plus docs/guide/index.html) and every real payload-mode file (all 21 build_payload_md_set() files) for `e.g.`, `i.e.`, `etc.`, `vs.`, `cf.` co-located with any of `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, `tools/` found several isolated single-directory mentions near an abbreviation (e.g. standard/NDS.md:109, standard/GIP.md:35/134) but ZERO instances of an abbreviation appearing BETWEEN two directory tokens inside a genuine payload/drift 3-or-4-directory enumeration, anywhere in the corpus. Per the stated fairness bar, this is a constructible-but-never-in-docs edge case, not a corpus-relevant or realistic-governance-prose defect -- correctly an observation, not a blocking issue. Should a future edit ever insert such phrasing into a real enumeration, it would be a genuine miss, but nothing in the current corpus or contracted fixtures exercises it.

observations.topic: Normalization side effects (task item 3) -- all three sub-probes independently verified sound on the real corpus
observations.detail:
(a) Token fabrication/destruction: grepped docs/guide/index.html (the corpus's only file with inline tags around directory tokens) for a tag boundary splitting a directory token from its trailing slash (e.g. `tools/`) -- none found; every real occurrence consistently wraps the full token INCLUDING the slash inside one tag (`tools/`, verified at lines 361-362, 383, 426-427, 438-439, 441, 445, 448, 519, 546, 554, 562), so tag-stripping-to-space can neither fabricate nor destroy a directory token anywhere in the real corpus. (b) The guide's ASCII directory-tree `
` block (lines 406-412) was directly extracted post-normalization: it merges with its preceding 'What you get:' lead-in and the following '--verify-only confirms...' paragraph into one large segment (because none of the intervening code-block/colon-delimited text contains a real `.`+whitespace terminator until far later) -- but that merged segment legitimately contains `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, and `tools/` all together, confirmed directly against the real file's segmented output, so it correctly PASSes; it does not fragment into a tools/-omitting piece. (c) Scanned the entire guide file with a regex for any literal `<`/`>` used in prose (not as a tag) and for any tag whose closing `>` falls on a different physical line than its opening `<` (which `<[^>]*>` -- a negated class that DOES match newlines -- would still handle correctly, but was checked for completeness): zero literal-bracket-in-prose occurrences and zero multi-line tags found in the real corpus, so no tag-strip mishandling case is currently exercised. NOTE (non-blocking, logged for awareness): the same 'What you get:'/code-block merge mechanism in (b) is a genuinely new merge SHAPE introduced by the redesign (colon-led-ins and terminator-free code blocks bridging paragraphs, rather than rev0's blank-line-paragraph join or rev2's `
` tag boundary) -- it happens to be benign today only because the merged run's content is not stale, the same 'benign by content coincidence, not structural guarantee' caveat the rev2 reviewer raised about the card-grid block. A full corpus+fixture re-run (task item 4 below) found this introduces no actual false-pass on the current tree, so it is not a blocking defect, but is worth tracking if the guide's code-block-adjacent prose is edited in the future.

observations.topic: Non-breaking gate independently re-verified across the FULL reconstructed corpus (task item 4)
observations.detail:
Reconstructed both file sets directly from tools/validate.sh's own build_shipped_md_set() and build_payload_md_set() function bodies (not trusted from contract prose): the real 37-file shipped set + docs/guide/index.html = 38 files for default mode, and the real 21-file build_payload_md_set() output for payload mode. Ran the REV3 oracle (frontmatter-strip -> normalize -> segment -> vlib_no_stale_payload) against every file in both sets. WITHOUT the standard/GIP.md content fix: exactly 1 failure (standard/GIP.md itself) in BOTH default mode and payload mode -- matching the contract's claimed 'exactly one failure ... proving the fix is genuinely forced' precisely. WITH the GIP.md fix applied in-memory only (never touching the tracked file): 0 failures in BOTH modes. Independently confirmed docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md's wrapped 4-dir enumeration PASSes, and docs/guide/index.html's own `

` enumeration and `

` ASCII tree both PASS (see item 3(b) above for the tree's merge detail). CONTEXT.md and tools/interface.md also independently confirmed to PASS sub-check (1) with no false-flag.

observations.topic: New stale_payload_html.html fixture and prior regression fixtures re-verified content-driven (task item 5)
observations.detail:
Reconstructed tools/fixtures/stale_payload_html.html exactly as described in scope.files_create (a stale `

` enumeration immediately followed, same source line, no blank line, by an unrelated `

` tools/ mention) and ran it through the oracle: FAILs, tagged with the reconstructed offending segment naming only the stale sentence's directory tokens; the unrelated 'unrelated utility scripts' text never appears in the offending output, matching the contract's own anti-cheat verification design exactly. Since the detection is implemented as a pure python3 content transform with no filename branching anywhere in the described design, the 'content-driven, not filename-keyed' anti-cheat claims for all five stale_payload_* fixtures are structurally guaranteed by the mechanism's own design, not merely asserted. The longwrap/semicolon/cosentence fixtures, independently reconstructed from their scope.files_create descriptions, still correctly guard their respective rev0/rev1 defect classes under the rev3 mechanism (see item 1 above).

observations.topic: python3/regex well-formedness and vacuous-pre-pass check (task item 6)
observations.detail: Directly tested the normalize+segment python3 stage against an empty file, a whitespace-only file, and a frontmatter-only (no-body) file, each invoked under an explicit `set -euo pipefail` bash subshell: all three exit 0 with no abort, confirming the PIPEFAIL SAFETY claim (no grep/head/cut pipeline in this stage whose legitimate 'no output' exit code could trip pipefail). Ran all 27 real verification commands from .agent/contracts/027.json (rev3) against the CURRENT, genuinely-unimplemented tree (no check_c10_consistency function exists yet; none of the 7 new fixture files exist yet; the 3 content fixes are unapplied). Exactly 4 entries legitimately PASS pre-implementation for correct 'nothing broke yet' reasons: index 3 (AT3, validate.sh still exits 0), index 9 (AT9, scope guard clean since nothing changed), index 10 (AT10, C1-C9 all still PASS), and index 27 (non-goal enforcement, NIZAM.json/CHANGELOG/etc. still untouched). All 23 remaining entries correctly FAIL (missing check function, missing fixtures, unfixed guide/methodology/GIP content, no `*.html` target case). No vacuous pre-pass detected.

Sub-checks (2) discovery-order and (3) version-anchor are unmodified across rev0->rev3 per design_notes; spot-re-confirmed directly against the real tree: CONTEXT.md still contains neither canonical discovery-order label ('bootstrapped-consumer discovery' / 'framework-checkout fallback'), preserving the P3 scoping assumption; NIZAM.json's `framework.version` is still `0.5.1`; docs/guide/index.html's `<meta name="framework-version">` and `<span id="footer-version">` anchors are both still `0.5.1`, matching the External Anchor Rule's fixed comparison point; tools/validate.sh's --target switch independently re-confirmed to have no pre-existing `*.html)` case, confirming the new case this contract adds is genuinely additive, not a duplicate/conflicting registration.

final_verdict.summary:
APPROVED at revision 3 (the human-authorized format-agnostic redesign, attempt 4 of the circuit-breaker's extended attempts=3/limit=5 window). Built an independent oracle strictly from design_notes' REV3 prose (frontmatter-strip -> python3 tag-strip-to-space -> whitespace-collapse -> `re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+')` sentence segmentation -> per-segment invocation of the REAL, unmodified vlib_no_stale_payload) and adversarially dry-ran it against the actual repository and reconstructed fixtures, not merely reasoned about the design. All three prior rejection classes are genuinely and independently re-confirmed closed: the rev0 co-located-sentence false-pass, the rev1 long-wrap and semicolon false-negatives, and the rev2 HTML `

`-collapsed false-pass -- the last of these, which drove the redesign mandate, is closed by construction (tag-to-space replacement exposes the `.`+whitespace sentence boundary regardless of original physical-line layout), not by a further ad hoc boundary-rule patch, matching the human's stated mandate to end the format-edge whack-a-mole. The full 38-file default corpus and 21-file payload corpus were independently reconstructed directly from tools/validate.sh's own build_shipped_md_set()/build_payload_md_set() function bodies and re-verified non-breaking exactly as claimed: 0 sub-check-1 failures in both modes with the standard/GIP.md content fix applied in-memory, and exactly 1 failure (GIP.md itself) in each mode without it, proving the fix is genuinely forced. Two NEW risk classes specific to this redesign were adversarially probed per the task brief: (1) sentence-segmentation splitting on abbreviation-period-plus-whitespace (e.g., i.e., etc., vs., cf.) was confirmed to be a real mechanism-level false-negative in isolation, but an exhaustive corpus grep found zero instances of an abbreviation appearing between two payload-directory tokens inside any real enumeration anywhere in the default or payload file sets -- per the stated fairness bar this is a constructible-but-never-in-docs edge case and is correctly logged as an observation, not a blocking issue. (2) Normalization side effects (tag-strip token fabrication/destruction, the guide's `
` ASCII-tree segment, and tag/prose bracket-character mishandling) were each independently probed against the real docs/guide/index.html and found sound: no real occurrence of a directory token split across a tag boundary, the ASCII tree's merged segment legitimately contains all four directory tokens together, and no literal `<`/`>`-in-prose or multi-line-tag case exists in the real corpus. The python3 normalize/segment stage was confirmed to exit 0 on empty/whitespace-only/frontmatter-only input under `set -euo pipefail`, and all 27 real verification commands were run against the current unimplemented tree, confirming exactly the 4 expected pre-implementation passes with no vacuous pre-pass among the remaining 23. Sub-checks (2)/(3), the External Anchor Rule, P3 scoping, the new `*.html` --target case, the scope guard, and 3-mode pinning are all independently re-confirmed unchanged and sound. No blocking issue, no missing acceptance coverage, and no unsupported claim was found. This contract is ready to proceed to implementation.
